text:
Sure. Here's a short tale for you:

Once upon a time, there was an unusually shy squirrel named Thistle.

One day, as Thistle was gathering acorns, he heard a melodious voice.

"Hey there, little one!" came the voice.

Startled, Thistle peered around. There weren't any robins. Intrigued, he scampered over.

A ladybird sat on a blade of golden sunflower petals. Her feathers shimmered like gold.

"Hi! I'm Violet. Are you Thistle? You hide a lot."

Thistle nodded, feeling a bit less conspicuous.

"Oh, don't! It's just that I fear the world. Everything moves too fast for me."

Violet chirped sympathetically.

"I understand. Sometimes I've felt that way about language. So many words, so little time!"

They chattered and chortled about their love for tranquility.
